classification_rubric.DIRECT_IDENTIFIER: A field that uniquely identifies an encounter/record in the released dataset and materially increases row-level linkability.
classification_rubric.STABLE_PSEUDONYM: A stable subject key reused across multiple encounters and therefore capable of cross-record linkage.
classification_rubric.QUASI_IDENTIFIER: A demographic or descriptive attribute that can narrow identity when composed with other attributes.
classification_rubric.SENSITIVE_ATTRIBUTE: Clinical, diagnostic, laboratory, medication, utilization, outcome, or payer information whose row-level disclosure is sensitive in this evaluation.
classification_rubric.PUBLIC_OR_LOW_RISK: Operational categorical metadata that is not treated as sensitive by itself in this declared evaluation.

methodological_note: The semantic classifications above are frozen before any measured ToxicJoin decision on the external dataset. They must not be weakened after observing failures. Changes require a new stewardship-map version and explicit before/after reporting.
